description: In July 2011, Netflix announced a major pricing restructure, splitting its DVD-by-mail and streaming services into separate plans and effectively raising prices for subscribers by up to 60%. The move caused massive customer backlash and stock price collapse, forcing CEO Reed Hastings to eventually cancel the 'Qwikster' brand spin-off.

key_events.description: Netflix announces split of DVD and streaming services.
Social media outrage reaches peak, 'Dear Netflix' blog post goes viral.
Netflix stock drops significantly; management apologizes.
Company scraps the Qwikster brand and maintains unified billing for existing members.

calibration_notes: A classic case study in brand damage caused by sudden, poorly communicated price increases that ignore user loyalty.
